title: .senpai/state.json
description: Documents the field set scripts/state-store.js reads and writes. Not wired into runtime validation (state-store.js fails closed on parse errors instead); this is a reference contract for anyone editing state-store.js, scope-check.js, or senpai-approve.js.

session_id.description: Claude Code session_id that senpai-approve.js bound this approval to. approval-gate.js/scope-check.js must reject a match against any other session.

scope_hash.description: Hash of the approved file scope (scripts/scope-hash.js). Must match the current allowed_files list for isApprovalValid() to return true.

approved_at.description: ISO timestamp set by senpai-approve.js's recordApproval(), never by the model.

intent.description: Classified user intent from scripts/classify-intent.js (e.g. add_feature, debug, explain_nondev, start_project). Declared but never written (docs/P4_5_SECURITY_FIX_AND_STUB_AUDIT.md M3) -- classify-intent.js's CLI form only prints to stdout for that turn; skills re-derive intent from the raw message each turn instead of reading it back.

meeting.description: Meeting type selected by scripts/select-meeting.js, or null for a non-meeting flow (debug, explain_nondev). Same as intent: declared but never written, re-derived each turn from conversation context and vault documents.

understanding_state.description: Free-form marker of how far the current meeting has progressed. Declared but never written -- guided-auto-drive/SKILL.md uses this as a conversational vocabulary the model judges each turn, not a value it reads back. Consequence (intentional, not a bug): hooks/scripts/handler.js#deriveMeetingStateHints() gates buildApproved on `['user_confirmed', 'decision_confirmed'].includes(state.understanding_state)`, which can never be true since no writer ever sets this field -- so buildApproved is structurally always false on the hook-nudge path, and selectMeeting() can never return build_readiness_meeting through that path. Build Readiness Meeting is reached instead via a separate skill path: guided-auto-drive's step 7 (Guided Work) calls `guided-plan` directly, bypassing this formula entirely and judging readiness from conversation context and vault documents (Unknown Map.md/Decision Index.md) instead of state.json. See docs/09_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA.md section 5 and docs/06_HOOKS_SPEC.md's UserPromptSubmit correction notice.

unresolved_decisions.description: Intended to list decisions surfaced but not yet confirmed by the user. No code path currently writes this field (Fable 5 stub-audit finding, P4.5) -- meeting-system's build-readiness hint formula (hooks/scripts/handler.js#deriveMeetingStateHints(), documented identically in skills/meeting-system/SKILL.md's step 2) reads it as a number (`(state.unresolved_decisions ?? 1) > 0`), a type mismatch against this array schema, and is unreachable at runtime either way (see the understanding_state field above for why). Documented as-is rather than silently dropped; not yet wired to a real writer.

approved_scope.description: The single field that flips build-write gating from deny to allow/ask (T0-T3 model, 2026-07 redesign). Set ONLY by senpai-approve.js's recordApproval(), triggered ONLY by a [senpai-go:<project>] UserPromptSubmit phrase whose <project> matches the pending Phase Plan's own project folder.

allowed_files.description: Repo-root-relative paths approved for writing, derived from the pending Phase Plan's YAML frontmatter -- never from the approval trigger message itself.

sensitive_files.description: Files touching a require_approval_for category (auth/payment/deploy/db/dependency-install/destructive) -- the Phase Plan's own frontmatter flags PLUS scope-check.js's hardcoded escalate-only pattern floor (matchesSensitiveFloor; the Phase Plan can only add to this, never remove a floor match). Written by senpai-approve.js's recordApproval() from the Phase Plan's sensitive_files frontmatter (T2). scope-check.js's checkPathsAgainstScope denies a write matching this list until the matching entry is also in confirmed_sensitive_files, even when the scope is otherwise approved; the rest of allowed_files (T1) is 'allow' with no re-confirmation.

confirmed_sensitive_files.description: Independent review finding (2026-07, HIGH/MAJOR/CONFIRMED): T2 used to return 'ask', proven unreliable under permissions.defaultMode:acceptEdits (the same signal the whole T0-T3 redesign exists to route around). Fixed: a sensitive_files write now denies until the user sends [senpai-touch:<project>:<file>] (UserPromptSubmit-based, immune to permission mode). Written one file at a time by senpai-approve.js's recordSensitiveFileConfirmation(); reset to [] by every fresh recordApproval(). Never grants scope on its own -- only consulted for a target checkPathsAgainstScope has already independently confirmed is in both allowed_files and sensitive_files.

verification_targets.description: Build/test command strings the approved Phase Plan declared for verification (P4.5 C2). Written by senpai-approve.js's recordApproval() from the Phase Plan's verification_commands frontmatter (filtered by a JS-toolchain-prefix sanity check, not a security boundary). NEVER auto-executed via Bash -- a security review (P4.5) found a prefix filter cannot safely bound a Turing-complete runner (npm/node/etc can run arbitrary code), so scope-check.js does not grant Bash access based on this field. evidence-loop reads it only to ask the human to run these commands in their own terminal and report the result back. Empty array means the plan declared nothing to suggest.

evidence_status.description: Free-form status of the evidence-loop check for the current scope. Declared but never written -- evidence-loop/SKILL.md re-reads the project's Completion Evidence.md vault document each turn instead.

pending_phase_plan_path.description: Repo-root-relative path to the Phase Plan awaiting [senpai-go:<project>]. Written in-process by scope-check.js's checkToolCall as a side effect of allowing a vault write to a file named "Phase Plan.md" -- NOT by guided-plan itself (a model has no reachable way to write to .senpai/state.json directly). Read by senpai-approve.js to derive allowed_files/sensitive_files/verification_targets, and to validate the <project> the user typed.
